topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Overcontrol pattern (Coping)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Well, I mean, I guess I do try to manage things. It’s just, you know, life can get a bit overwhelming sometimes, and I feel like if I don’t keep a tight grip on things, then everything might just fall apart. But then again, maybe I overthink it? I don’t know. It’s just that when things don’t go as planned, like with that insurance claim, it makes me feel like I should have done better. So, yeah, maybe I do try to control things too much. But it’s not like I want to be that way. It just happens, I suppose.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Academic failure (Education)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Well, I mean, school was always a bit of a mixed bag for me. I did okay, but there were times when I felt like I was just barely keeping my head above water. I guess I always had this nagging feeling that I needed to prove myself, you know? Like if I didn’t get good grades or if I stumbled on a project, it would reflect poorly on me. So, yeah, maybe there’s something there. It’s like I’ve carried that pressure into other areas of my life, and when things don’t go smoothly, it just amplifies that fear of not being good enough. It’s exhausting, really. I just want to feel like I’m doing things right without constantly second-guessing myself.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Humiliation (Trauma)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Yeah, I think that’s definitely part of it. When I mess up or something doesn’t go the way I hoped, it’s like this wave of shame just crashes over me. I feel embarrassed, like I’ve let myself down, and then I start to worry about what others might think too. It’s like I can’t escape that feeling of being judged, even if it’s just in my own head. I mean, I know everyone makes mistakes, but for me, it feels like it’s magnified. I just keep replaying those moments in my mind, thinking about how I could have done better. It’s exhausting, and it makes it hard to just move on and focus on the next thing. I guess I just want to be able to accept that I’m not perfect and that it’s okay to mess up sometimes, but it’s so hard to let go of that pressure.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Psychiatric medication (Mental Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Client: Daisy: You know, I’ve thought about it a bit, but it’s kind of a mixed bag for me. I mean, on one hand, I get that medication can help some people, and I’ve seen friends who’ve benefited from it. But then there’s this part of me that feels like I should be able to handle things on my own, like if I take medication, it’s somehow admitting defeat or that I’m not strong enough. I guess I worry about becoming reliant on something external instead of figuring it out myself. But at the same time, the anxiety can be so overwhelming that I wonder if it might be worth considering. It’s just a lot to think about, and I’m not sure what the right choice is. I feel like I’m stuck in this loop of wanting to be independent but also feeling like I need help, which is frustrating.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Stress overload (Mental Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Yeah, I think that makes sense. The stress definitely feels like it's piling up, especially with everything I've been trying to manage on my own. Between school, finances, and just trying to figure out how to be an adult, it can feel like a lot. I mean, I want to be independent, but sometimes it feels like I’m juggling too many balls in the air, and I’m just waiting for one of them to drop. It’s like I have this constant pressure to keep everything together, and when something goes wrong, it just adds to that feeling of being overwhelmed. I guess I’m just trying to navigate all these responsibilities while also dealing with the anxiety that comes with it, and it’s exhausting. I really want to find a way to manage it all without feeling like I’m drowning, you know?

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Financial dependency (Economy)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Yeah, that definitely resonates with me. I mean, I’ve always felt this strong urge to be independent, especially after seeing my brother still relying on our parents. I really don’t want to end up in that position, so I’m pushing myself to handle everything on my own. But then, when I think about finances, it’s like this constant reminder of how new and tricky it all is for me. I feel like I’m trying to prove that I can do it, but every little hiccup just makes me question if I’m really capable. It’s frustrating because I want to be proud of my independence, but instead, it feels like this heavy weight that I’m carrying around. I guess I just want to feel secure in my ability to manage things without that nagging fear of failing or needing help. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of wanting to show I can do it while also feeling overwhelmed by the pressure to succeed.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Trauma response (Mental Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: Yeah, I think that’s a really interesting idea. Redefining independence sounds like it could be helpful, but it’s hard to know where to start. I mean, I’ve always thought of independence as doing everything on my own and not needing anyone else, but maybe it doesn’t have to be that way. I guess I could think about it more as being able to ask for help when I need it without feeling like I’m failing. It’s just that it feels so ingrained in me to handle things solo, and even the thought of reaching out for support makes me anxious. I worry that if I start leaning on others, I might lose that sense of control I’ve been trying to build. But then again, if I keep pushing myself to do everything alone, I just end up feeling overwhelmed and stuck. It’s like I’m caught in this tug-of-war between wanting to be strong and realizing that maybe asking for help doesn’t mean I’m weak. I just need to figure out how to navigate that without feeling like I’m compromising my independence.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Body image distress (Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Daisy: I think sharing my experiences with friends could definitely help ease some of that pressure. I mean, just knowing that they’re going through similar things makes me feel less isolated, you know? It’s like we’re all in this together, and talking about it might lighten the load a bit. I guess I’ve always been hesitant to open up, thinking I should just handle everything myself, but maybe it’s time to let them in a little. I worry about burdening them, though, or that they might not understand the extent of what I’m feeling. But then again, if I don’t share, I’m just keeping all this weight on my shoulders. It’s a bit of a balancing act, I guess. I just hope that if I do start talking about it, I won’t feel like I’m losing control or that I’m not capable anymore. It’s just hard to shift that mindset, but I think it could be a good step toward feeling a bit lighter.
